design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate vertically scrolling stages, destroy enemy formations, collect weapon/power pickups, manage firepower and bomb resources across 10 escalating levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Bullet density and enemy placement difficulty increases with each loop; player must balance aggressive offense (to maintain power) against defensive survival (firepower loss on hit)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone fighter pilot commanding a super-powered biplane against overwhelming military armada; progression from defending against initial invasion to countering a mysterious global threat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict, unforgiving arcade design philosophy that prioritizes difficulty escalation and score-chasing over accessibility; developer explicitly regretted not balancing difficulty for broader appeal; loops endlessly with increasing bullet patterns rather than soft conclusion

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives earned through reaching specific score thresholds; single hit causes life loss and firepower/speed reset to default state
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade version supports credit insertion to continue; console version behavior not specified
difficulty.checkpointing: Configurable arcade setting: either checkpoint-based restart at stage beginning or respawn at death location
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; players can choose weapon pickups (wide shot, laser, flamethrower) and prioritize speed/bomb items, but difficulty scaling is entirely designer-controlled and loops indefinitely

progression.upgrade_system: Three-tier firepower system (collected via 'P' icons, three required per level); speed increases via 'S' icons; bomb stock via 'B' icons; once fully powered, additional P icons convert to points
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stages unlock sequentially through completion; no branching paths. Game loops back to stage 1 after stage 10, each loop increasing enemy density and bullet patterns
replayability_hooks: Endless loop mechanic with escalating difficulty encourages high-score chasing
Score-based extra lives create arcade economy incentive
Multiple weapon types (wide shot, laser, flamethrower) allow different tactical approaches
Lightning-shaped medal collection for points adds risk/reward element

world_building_techniques: Arcade version: cryptic opening (Strange Fleet appears on Mediterranean island, grows over 2 years, launches worldwide assault)
Genesis version: alternate-Earth scenario with S Corps industrial superpower; phantom pilot mythology around Fire Shark biplane
Military aesthetics: tanks, battleships, kamikaze monoplanes, artillery create coherent enemy faction identity
Stage progression through geography implies advancing toward enemy stronghold

faction_indicators: Strange Fleet (arcade): mysterious armada from Mediterranean, described as unified global threat
S Corps (Genesis): industrial superpower conducting systematic invasion
Player faction implied: those who 'oppose them' call for Fire Shark pilot intervention
danger_signals: Sudden, covert arrival of Strange Fleet on small island before global expansion
Dense enemy formations and escalating bullet patterns signal militaristic threat escalation
Kamikaze monoplanes and artillery indicate desperation/extremism of enemy forces
environmental_storytelling: Scrolling backdrop reveals military infrastructure targets (airports, battleship formations, industrial facilities)
Airport checkpoints serve as safe zones between combat sections
Mediterranean island setting grounds conflict in specific geopolitical region
Enemy unit types (tanks, battleships) reveal conventional military doctrine vs. player's unconventional biplane approach
